documentation.text:
@dev Aggregate submissions for a round into a single encrypted price.
      Simple approach (Wave 2): take first submission.
      Full approach (Wave 3): compute encrypted median via FHE comparisons.
      The median algorithm works as follows without decrypting:
        1. For each pair (i, j) of submitted prices, compute FHE.gt(p_i, p_j).
        2. Count how many prices each submission is greater than.
        3. The median is the price where count ≈ n/2.
      This is O(n²) FHE operations — feasible for n ≤ 5 feeders per round.
@notice Metadata for a feed — no price exposure.
@notice How many feeders have submitted in the current (pending) round.

@title MockConsumer
 @notice Demo consumer contract for Wave 2 evaluation.
 Shows how any DeFi protocol integrates with FHEOracleBridge:
   1. This contract is whitelisted in AccessRegistry.
   2. It pulls euint256 price from the oracle.
   3. It performs comparisons in FHE — no plaintext price ever exposed.
 Demo scenarios:
   - isPriceAbove(threshold)     → liquidation trigger check
   - isPriceBelow(threshold)     → buy signal check
   - isWithinBand(lower, upper)  → range check for AMM
@notice Check if price is below threshold (e.g. buy signal).

@notice Check if price is within a band [lower, upper].
         Used for AMM range orders and structured products.

@title PrivateLiquidator
 @notice Wave 4 deliverable — real consumer that integrates with FHEOracleBridge.
 This contract manages collateralised positions and liquidates them when
 the encrypted oracle price falls below the encrypted liquidation threshold.
 The key insight: at no point does any plaintext price appear in a transaction.
 - Price enters the oracle as an FHE ciphertext from feeders.
 - This contract pulls it as euint256.
 - The comparison is done in FHE.
 - Only the boolean result (liquidate: yes/no) is revealed.
 This is the demo moment for judges: one tx sequence, zero price exposure.

@notice Liquidate an undercollateralised position.
         Anyone can call this — liquidators are incentivised by a 5% reward.
@notice Close a healthy position and reclaim collateral.
@notice Wrap a plaintext uint256 as a mock euint256.
@notice Return a zero-valued mock euint256 (uninitialised feed price).

@notice Mock FHE greater-than. Returns true if a > b.
         On Fhenix: replace with FHE.gt(a, b) which returns ebool.

@notice Mock FHE select (ternary). Returns a if cond, else b.
         On Fhenix: replace with FHE.select(cond, a, b).
